summary: GT Bray Recreation Center in Bradenton offers summer camps for kids with free lunches and snacks through a school district partnership.

title: Braden Castle ruins in Bradenton mark the site where a sugar plantation once stood
summary: The ruins of Braden Castle in Bradenton tell the story of a sugar plantation, a Seminole attack, and a community that still thrives today.

title: Thousands of people cruise to Anna Maria Island on Bradenton’s Gulf Islands Ferry
summary: The Gulf Islands Ferry connects Bradenton's Riverwalk to Anna Maria Island in 45 minutes. Tickets start at $11 for adults; kids under four ride free.

title: LECOM Ball Park in Bradenton builds community connections on and off the baseball field
summary: LECOM Ball Park in Bradenton is expanding its community presence with 100-plus local events in 2025 and a new city park breaking ground in June.

title: The Nest at Robinson Preserve offers families trails, kayaking, and treetop views in Manatee County
summary: Robinson Preserve's Nest Nature Center in Manatee County offers 500 annual programs, a shaded playground, and 20-plus miles of trails and kayaking routes.

title: From wilderness settlement to riverfront city, Bradenton's history spans more than 180 years
summary: From a wilderness settlement in 1842 to a thriving riverfront city, Bradenton's history is still visible in its streets, structures, and ballpark.

title: Two teens in stolen vehicle rescued after trying to swim across Manatee River: MCSO
summary: Two teenagers driving a stolen vehicle were arrested on Wednesday after trying to escape by swimming across the Manatee River, authorities said.
